response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and valuable geospatial domain knowledge from GIS and mapping work, along with solid Linux/backend systems experience. However, critical skill gaps undermine the fit: C++ expertise is outdated (last used 2014-2015), with no Rust experience. The past decade of career focus on web/full-stack development (JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, React) represents a substantial move away from systems-level programming required for this role. While the demonstrated ability to master multiple technologies provides potential, a mid-senior IC role typically expects someone productive in the primary technology without extensive ramp-up.
strengths: Proven geospatial and GIS domain expertise through Green Map and Amazon open-source geo-data platform projects
Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals demonstrated across roles (TDD, CI/CD, clean architecture, distributed systems experience)
Extensive Linux backend and distributed systems experience, including micro-services handling millions of requests per second
concerns: C++ expertise is significantly outdated, last used professionally in 2014-2015; no recent systems-level programming experience
No demonstrated experience with navigation/routing systems, routing algorithms, or data pipeline optimization
Recent 10-year career trajectory focused on web/full-stack development (JavaScript/TypeScript) shows movement away from core role requirements

key_factors.factor: Outdated C++ expertise with 10+ year gap since last use
Career trajectory away from systems programming toward web/application development
Relevant geospatial/GIS domain knowledge from prior projects
